channel.title: Exploring Kubernetes
channel.description: A progressive journey through Kubernetes, from your first deployment to production operations.

item.title: Home
item.description: A practical journey through Kubernetes for developers and platform engineers. Learn core concepts, kubectl, and Helm through real-world scenarios.

item.title: Overview
item.description: Developer-focused introduction to Kubernetes. Learn what K8s is, deploy your first application, and master essential kubectl commands with confidence.

item.title: What Is Kubernetes?
item.description: An easy-to-understand introduction to Kubernetes (K8s) for beginners. Learn about container orchestration, why it exists, and the benefits of using it.

item.title: Getting Helm Access
item.description: Install Helm, the Kubernetes package manager, and connect it to your cluster. Step-by-step guide for macOS, Linux, and Windows with troubleshooting tips.

item.title: Essential Helm Commands
item.description: Master daily Helm commands: list, status, get values, upgrade, rollback. Covers when to use Helm vs kubectl, plus a real-world incident rollback walkthrough.

item.title: Your First Helm Deployment
item.description: Deploy your first Helm chart to Kubernetes. Covers CI/CD pipeline charts and vendor charts with values.yaml customization, upgrades, rollbacks, and cleanup.

item.title: Understanding What Helm Created
item.description: See how Helm translates values.yaml into running Kubernetes resources. Understand the rendering pipeline, release tracking, and how to troubleshoot each layer.

item.title: Getting kubectl Access
item.description: Install kubectl and configure Kubernetes cluster access using kubeconfig files. Complete setup guide for AWS, Azure, GCP, and on-premises clusters.

item.title: Essential kubectl Commands
item.description: Master essential kubectl commands for managing Pods, Deployments, and Services. Practical cheat sheet with examples for troubleshooting K8s applications.

item.title: Your First Deployment
item.description: Deploy your first containerized application to Kubernetes with kubectl. Step-by-step guide covering Pods, Services, and troubleshooting common issues.

item.title: Understanding What Happened
item.description: Deep dive into Kubernetes architecture. Learn how Deployments, ReplicaSets, and Pods work together when you deploy applications to K8s clusters.

item.title: The CRI: How kubelet Talks to Container Runtimes
item.description: How kubelet talks to containerd or CRI-O through the CRI: the interface, the dockershim history, and the full path from apply to a running container.

item.title: cert-manager: Certificates as Cluster Resources
item.description: Issuers, Certificates, and one Gateway annotation: how cert-manager turns TLS issuance into desired state — plus the debug chain for when it won't issue.

item.title: Pointing Your Domain at the Cluster with external-dns
item.description: The last manual step, automated: external-dns watches your HTTPRoutes and writes DNS records — to a cloud provider, or to authoritative DNS you run yourself.

item.title: Gateway API with Traefik: The Standard Front Door
item.description: Stop writing Ingress annotations. How Gateway API's role-split design works — GatewayClass, Gateway, HTTPRoute — wired end to end with Traefik, TLS included.

item.title: Cluster Architecture
item.description: A working mental model of a Kubernetes cluster — the control plane that decides, the nodes that run your Pods, and the reconcile loop that ties them together.

item.title: ConfigMaps and Secrets
item.description: How Kubernetes ConfigMaps and Secrets keep config out of images — env vars vs files, update propagation, and why a Secret is not encryption.

item.title: Deployments
item.description: How Kubernetes Deployments manage ReplicaSets to scale, self-heal, and roll out zero-downtime updates — the resource you actually create in production.

item.title: Kubernetes Ingress: Reading the Front Door You Inherit
item.description: Every existing cluster still routes through Ingress. Read rules, pathTypes, TLS, and annotations fluently — and plan the Gateway API migration off ingress-nginx.

item.title: Jobs and CronJobs
item.description: Run tasks to completion instead of forever — database migrations, scheduled backups, and batch processing with Kubernetes Jobs and CronJobs.
